---
title: "Wealth Building"
subtitle: "Financial planning for PhD students on a graduate stipend"
description: "Budgeting templates, retirement account options, student loan management, and credit-building strategies — all calibrated for a graduate school income."
order: 2
---

Building financial security on a graduate stipend is challenging but possible. The key is starting early — even small, consistent contributions to an emergency fund and retirement account compound significantly over a 5–7 year PhD program. This guide covers budgeting, saving, investing, and managing debt on a grad-school income.

---

## 1. Key Financial Milestones for Graduate Students

Work through these roughly in order:

1. **Build a $1,000 starter emergency fund** — protects against unexpected car repairs, medical bills, etc.
2. **Maximize employer retirement match** — if your university offers any match on a 403(b) or 457(b), contribute at least enough to capture the full match (free money).
3. **Pay off high-interest debt** (>7% APR) — credit cards and private student loans.
4. **Build a 3–6 month emergency fund** — equivalent to 3–6 months of essential expenses.
5. **Contribute to a Roth IRA** — up to $7,000/year (2024 limit) if you have earned income (TA/RA W-2). Fellowship income may also qualify — confirm with a tax advisor.
6. **Reduce or refinance moderate-interest debt** (undergraduate loans, if applicable).
7. **Increase retirement contributions** in a 403(b) / 457(b) if available.

---

## 2. Monthly Budget Template {#sec-budget-template}

> **💾 Cross-page linked.** City and stipend are loaded from [Salary & Cost of Living](salary_and_cost_of_living.qmd). Select a city and adjust your stipend there to populate city-specific estimates here.

Use the interactive budget below. **Check or uncheck line items** to include or exclude them from your budget. City-specific estimates automatically adjust based on the cost-of-living index for your selected city.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Load shared state from localStorage
bud_city_name = localStorage.getItem('phd_city') || 'Austin, TX'
bud_coli_raw  = parseFloat(localStorage.getItem('phd_city_coli') || '100')
bud_coli      = isFinite(bud_coli_raw) ? bud_coli_raw : 100
bud_stipend_raw = parseFloat(localStorage.getItem('phd_annual_stipend') || '30000')
bud_stipend   = isFinite(bud_stipend_raw) ? bud_stipend_raw : 30000
bud_monthly_net = Math.round((bud_stipend / 12) * 0.88)

// City-scaled budget line items
// Base amounts are for COLI=100 (Austin, TX); rent/transport/groceries scale with COLI
bud_lines_def = [
  // INCOME
  {id:"stipend",   label:"Net Stipend (after taxes)",              type:"income",  base: bud_monthly_net, scale:false, checked:true,  notes:"≈ stipend ÷ 12 × 0.88"},
  {id:"side",      label:"Side Income (tutoring, consulting…)",    type:"income",  base: 0,               scale:false, checked:false, notes:"Enter if applicable"},
  // NEEDS
  {id:"rent",      label:"Rent",                                   type:"needs",   base: 1050,            scale:true,  checked:true,  notes:"1BR; on-campus may be cheaper"},
  {id:"util",      label:"Utilities (electric, gas, internet)",    type:"needs",   base: 150,             scale:true,  checked:true,  notes:"Avg. $100–$200"},
  {id:"grocery",   label:"Groceries",                              type:"needs",   base: 380,             scale:true,  checked:true,  notes:"USDA moderate-cost plan"},
  {id:"transport", label:"Transportation",                         type:"needs",   base: 120,             scale:true,  checked:true,  notes:"Transit pass or car"},
  {id:"insurance", label:"Health Insurance Premium",               type:"needs",   base: 80,              scale:false, checked:true,  notes:"If not fully covered"},
  {id:"phone",     label:"Phone",                                  type:"needs",   base: 60,              scale:false, checked:true,  notes:"Budget carriers from ~$25"},
  {id:"loans",     label:"Minimum Loan Payments",                  type:"needs",   base: 0,               scale:false, checked:false, notes:"Undergrad debt if applicable"},
  // WANTS
  {id:"dining",    label:"Dining Out & Coffee",                    type:"wants",   base: 120,             scale:true,  checked:true,  notes:""},
  {id:"entertain", label:"Entertainment & Streaming",              type:"wants",   base: 60,              scale:false, checked:true,  notes:"Netflix, Spotify, etc."},
  {id:"clothing",  label:"Clothing & Personal Care",               type:"wants",   base: 60,              scale:true,  checked:true,  notes:""},
  {id:"hobbies",   label:"Hobbies",                                type:"wants",   base: 40,              scale:false, checked:true,  notes:""},
  // SAVINGS
  {id:"emerg",     label:"Emergency Fund Contribution",            type:"savings", base: 100,             scale:false, checked:true,  notes:"Target 3–6 months expenses"},
  {id:"ira",       label:"Roth IRA Contribution",                  type:"savings", base: 100,             scale:false, checked:true,  notes:"Max $583/mo ($7k/yr)"},
  {id:"ret403",    label:"403(b) / 457(b) Contribution",           type:"savings", base: 0,               scale:false, checked:false, notes:"If your university offers it"},
  {id:"debt_ex",   label:"Extra Debt Repayment",                   type:"savings", base: 0,               scale:false, checked:false, notes:"Above minimum payments"},
]
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Build checkbox table — HTML form; OJS reactive on checked IDs
viewof bud_checked = {
  const typeColors = {income:"#27ae60", needs:"#e74c3c", wants:"#3498db", savings:"#8e44ad"};
  const typeBg     = {income:"rgba(39,174,96,.08)", needs:"rgba(231,76,60,.07)", wants:"rgba(52,152,219,.07)", savings:"rgba(142,68,173,.07)"};
  const groups = ["income","needs","wants","savings"];
  const groupLabels = {income:"💵 Income", needs:"🏠 Needs", wants:"🎉 Wants", savings:"💰 Savings & Investments"};

  const checked = new Set(bud_lines_def.filter(l => l.checked).map(l => l.id));
  const container = document.createElement('div');
  container.style.cssText = 'font-family:inherit;';

  for (const grp of groups) {
    const lines = bud_lines_def.filter(l => l.type === grp);
    const section = document.createElement('div');
    section.style.cssText = `margin-bottom:8px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;overflow:hidden;`;
    section.innerHTML = `<div style="padding:8px 12px;font-weight:700;font-size:.88rem;background:${typeBg[grp]};color:${typeColors[grp]};border-bottom:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);">${groupLabels[grp]}</div>`;
    const table = document.createElement('table');
    table.style.cssText = 'width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.86rem;';
    for (const l of lines) {
      const scaled = l.scale ? Math.round(l.base * (bud_coli / 100)) : l.base;
      const tr = document.createElement('tr');
      tr.style.borderBottom = '1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6)';
      tr.innerHTML = `
        <td style="padding:6px 10px;width:30px;">
          <input type="checkbox" id="cb_${l.id}" ${checked.has(l.id) ? 'checked' : ''} style="cursor:pointer;">
        </td>
        <td style="padding:6px 4px;"><label for="cb_${l.id}" style="cursor:pointer;">${l.label}</label></td>
        <td style="padding:6px 8px;text-align:right;font-weight:600;">$${scaled.toLocaleString()}</td>
        <td style="padding:6px 10px;opacity:.6;font-size:.78rem;">${l.notes}</td>`;
      const cb = tr.querySelector('input');
      cb.addEventListener('change', () => {
        if (cb.checked) checked.add(l.id); else checked.delete(l.id);
        container.dispatchEvent(new Event('input'));
      });
      table.appendChild(tr);
    }
    section.appendChild(table);
    container.appendChild(section);
  }
  container.value = checked;
  container.dispatchEvent = (ev) => { HTMLElement.prototype.dispatchEvent.call(container, ev); };
  return container;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Compute totals from checked items
bud_active = bud_lines_def.map(l => ({
  ...l,
  amount: l.type === "income"
    ? (l.id === "stipend" ? bud_monthly_net : (bud_checked.has ? (bud_checked.has(l.id) ? l.base : 0) : 0))
    : (bud_checked.has ? (bud_checked.has(l.id) ? (l.scale ? Math.round(l.base * bud_coli / 100) : l.base) : 0) : 0),
  included: bud_checked.has ? bud_checked.has(l.id) : l.checked
}))

bud_total_income  = bud_active.filter(l => l.type === "income"  && l.included).reduce((s,l) => s+l.amount, 0)
bud_total_needs   = bud_active.filter(l => l.type === "needs"   && l.included).reduce((s,l) => s+l.amount, 0)
bud_total_wants   = bud_active.filter(l => l.type === "wants"   && l.included).reduce((s,l) => s+l.amount, 0)
bud_total_savings = bud_active.filter(l => l.type === "savings" && l.included).reduce((s,l) => s+l.amount, 0)
bud_net_cashflow  = bud_total_income - bud_total_needs - bud_total_wants - bud_total_savings

html`<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(130px,1fr));gap:8px;margin:10px 0;">
  <div style="padding:12px 8px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.05em;opacity:.6;">City</div>
    <div style="font-size:.95rem;font-weight:700;margin-top:3px;">${bud_city_name}</div>
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;opacity:.6;">COLI ${bud_coli}</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:12px 8px;border:2px solid #27ae60;border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.05em;opacity:.6;">Income</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.4rem;font-weight:700;color:#27ae60;margin-top:3px;">$${bud_total_income.toLocaleString()}</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:12px 8px;border:2px solid #e74c3c;border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.05em;opacity:.6;">Needs</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.4rem;font-weight:700;color:#e74c3c;margin-top:3px;">$${bud_total_needs.toLocaleString()}</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:12px 8px;border:2px solid #3498db;border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.05em;opacity:.6;">Wants</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.4rem;font-weight:700;color:#3498db;margin-top:3px;">$${bud_total_wants.toLocaleString()}</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:12px 8px;border:2px solid #8e44ad;border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.05em;opacity:.6;">Savings</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.4rem;font-weight:700;color:#8e44ad;margin-top:3px;">$${bud_total_savings.toLocaleString()}</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:12px 8px;border:2px solid ${bud_net_cashflow >= 0 ? '#27ae60' : '#e74c3c'};border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.05em;opacity:.6;">Net Cash Flow</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.4rem;font-weight:700;color:${bud_net_cashflow >= 0 ? '#27ae60' : '#e74c3c'};margin-top:3px;">${bud_net_cashflow >= 0 ? '+' : ''}$${bud_net_cashflow.toLocaleString()}</div>
  </div>
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Real-time stacked bar chart of budget allocation
bud_chart_data = [
  {category: "Needs",   amount: bud_total_needs,   pct: bud_total_income > 0 ? Math.round(bud_total_needs/bud_total_income*100) : 0},
  {category: "Wants",   amount: bud_total_wants,   pct: bud_total_income > 0 ? Math.round(bud_total_wants/bud_total_income*100) : 0},
  {category: "Savings", amount: bud_total_savings, pct: bud_total_income > 0 ? Math.round(bud_total_savings/bud_total_income*100) : 0},
  {category: "Unallocated", amount: Math.max(0, bud_net_cashflow), pct: bud_total_income > 0 ? Math.max(0, Math.round(bud_net_cashflow/bud_total_income*100)) : 0}
].filter(d => d.amount > 0)

Plot.plot({
  title: `Monthly Budget — ${bud_city_name} (COLI ${bud_coli})`,
  subtitle: "50/30/20 target: Needs ≤50%, Wants ≤30%, Savings ≥20%",
  width: 560,
  height: 200,
  marginLeft: 20,
  x: {label: "Monthly $ →", tickFormat: d => `$${d.toLocaleString()}`},
  color: {
    domain: ["Needs", "Wants", "Savings", "Unallocated"],
    range:  ["#e74c3c", "#3498db", "#8e44ad", "#bdc3c7"],
    legend: true
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(bud_chart_data, {
      x: "amount", fill: "category",
      title: d => `${d.category}: $${d.amount.toLocaleString()} (${d.pct}%)`,
      tip: true, rx: 4
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})
```

**Free budgeting tools:**

- [YNAB (You Need A Budget)](https://www.ynab.com/) — free for students
- [Mint / Credit Karma](https://www.creditkarma.com/)
- [Personal Capital (Empower)](https://www.empower.com/)

---

## 📊 Interactive Budget & Retirement Planner {#sec-budget-planner}

> **💾 Cross-page linked.** Stipend is loaded from [Salary & Cost of Living](salary_and_cost_of_living.qmd). Adjust below to override for this session.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
wb_stipend_init = parseFloat(localStorage.getItem('phd_annual_stipend') || '30000')

viewof wb_stipend = {
  const options = [15000,16000,17000,18000,19000,20000,21000,22000,23000,24000,25000,26000,27000,28000,29000,30000,31000,32000,33000,34000,35000,36000,37000,38000,39000,40000,42000,44000,46000,48000,50000,52000,54000,56000,58000,60000];
  const initVal = isFinite(wb_stipend_init) ? wb_stipend_init : 30000;
  const nearest = options.reduce((a,b) => Math.abs(b-initVal)<Math.abs(a-initVal)?b:a, 30000);
  const el = Inputs.select(options, {
    label: "Annual Stipend (pre-tax, $) — override if needed",
    format: d => `$${d.toLocaleString()}`,
    value: nearest
  });
  return el;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof wb_savings_pct = Inputs.select([5, 8, 10, 12, 15, 18, 20, 22, 25, 28, 30], {
  label: "Savings & Debt Repayment (% of take-home)",
  format: d => `${d}%`,
  value: 15
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof wb_ira_monthly = {
  const saved = parseFloat(localStorage.getItem('phd_ira_monthly') || '100');
  const options = [0, 25, 50, 75, 100, 125, 150, 200, 250, 300, 350, 400, 450, 500, 550, 583];
  const initSaved = isFinite(saved) ? saved : 100;
  const nearest = options.reduce((a,b) => Math.abs(b-initSaved)<Math.abs(a-initSaved)?b:a, 100);
  const el = Inputs.select(options, {
    label: "Monthly Roth IRA Contribution ($) — max $583/mo to hit $7,000/yr limit",
    format: d => `$${d}/mo`,
    value: nearest
  });
  el.addEventListener('change', () => localStorage.setItem('phd_ira_monthly', String(el.value)));
  return el;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// 0.88 ≈ 1 − 12% effective federal+state tax rate typical for grad-student income bracket
wb_monthly_net  = Math.round((wb_stipend / 12) * 0.88)
// Needs floor = 45% (minimum viable budget); 80 − savings_pct keeps needs + wants = 100 − savings
wb_needs_pct    = Math.max(45, 80 - wb_savings_pct)
wb_wants_pct    = 100 - wb_savings_pct - wb_needs_pct
wb_needs_amt    = Math.round(wb_monthly_net * wb_needs_pct  / 100)
wb_wants_amt    = Math.round(wb_monthly_net * wb_wants_pct  / 100)
wb_savings_amt  = Math.round(wb_monthly_net * wb_savings_pct / 100)

html`<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(140px,1fr));gap:10px;margin-top:14px;">
  <div style="padding:14px 10px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;opacity:.6">Take-Home/mo</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.5rem;font-weight:700;margin-top:4px;">$${wb_monthly_net}</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:14px 10px;border:2px solid #e74c3c;border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;opacity:.6">Needs (${wb_needs_pct}%)</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.5rem;font-weight:700;margin-top:4px;color:#e74c3c;">$${wb_needs_amt}</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:14px 10px;border:2px solid #3498db;border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;opacity:.6">Wants (${wb_wants_pct}%)</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.5rem;font-weight:700;margin-top:4px;color:#3498db;">$${wb_wants_amt}</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:14px 10px;border:2px solid #27ae60;border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.06em;opacity:.6">Savings (${wb_savings_pct}%)</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.5rem;font-weight:700;margin-top:4px;color:#27ae60;">$${wb_savings_amt}</div>
  </div>
</div>`
```

::: {.panel-tabset}

### 💰 Monthly Budget Breakdown

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
budget_categories = [
  {category: "Rent & Housing",        amount: Math.round(wb_needs_amt * 0.52), type: "Needs"},
  {category: "Groceries",             amount: Math.round(wb_needs_amt * 0.17), type: "Needs"},
  {category: "Utilities & Phone",     amount: Math.round(wb_needs_amt * 0.12), type: "Needs"},
  {category: "Transportation",        amount: Math.round(wb_needs_amt * 0.11), type: "Needs"},
  {category: "Health Insurance",      amount: Math.round(wb_needs_amt * 0.08), type: "Needs"},
  {category: "Dining & Coffee",       amount: Math.round(wb_wants_amt * 0.40), type: "Wants"},
  {category: "Entertainment & Subs.", amount: Math.round(wb_wants_amt * 0.32), type: "Wants"},
  {category: "Clothing & Personal",   amount: Math.round(wb_wants_amt * 0.28), type: "Wants"},
  {category: "Emergency Fund",        amount: Math.round(wb_savings_amt * 0.35), type: "Savings"},
  {category: "Roth IRA",              amount: Math.min(wb_ira_monthly, Math.round(wb_savings_amt * 0.50)), type: "Savings"},
  {category: "Debt Repayment",        amount: Math.round(wb_savings_amt * 0.15), type: "Savings"}
]

Plot.plot({
  title: "Monthly Budget Breakdown (50/30/20 framework)",
  width: 580,
  height: 360,
  marginLeft: 200,
  x: {label: "Monthly $ →"},
  color: {
    domain: ["Needs", "Wants", "Savings"],
    range: ["#e74c3c", "#3498db", "#27ae60"],
    legend: true
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(budget_categories, {
      x: "amount", y: "category", fill: "type",
      sort: {y: "x", reverse: true}, rx: 3, tip: true
    }),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})
```

### 📈 Roth IRA Growth Projection

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
ret_years = Array.from({length: 36}, (_, i) => i)
contrib_levels = [50, 100, wb_ira_monthly, 200, 300]
  .filter((v, i, a) => a.indexOf(v) === i)
  .sort((a, b) => a - b)

// 7% = long-run average annual real return of a diversified index-fund portfolio (historical US stock market)
compound_data = contrib_levels.flatMap(monthly =>
  ret_years.map(year => ({
    year,
    monthly,
    value: Math.round(monthly * ((Math.pow(1 + 0.07/12, year * 12) - 1) / (0.07/12))),
    label: `$${monthly}/mo`
  }))
)

Plot.plot({
  title: "Roth IRA Growth at 7% Average Annual Return",
  subtitle: "Assumes consistent monthly contributions; your selected amount highlighted.",
  width: 600,
  height: 320,
  x: {label: "Years of Contributions →"},
  y: {label: "Portfolio Value ($) →", tickFormat: d => `$${(d/1000).toFixed(0)}k`},
  color: {
    domain: contrib_levels.map(m => `$${m}/mo`),
    legend: true
  },
  marks: [
    Plot.line(compound_data, {
      x: "year", y: "value",
      stroke: d => `$${d.monthly}/mo`,
      strokeWidth: d => d.monthly === wb_ira_monthly ? 3.5 : 1.5,
      strokeOpacity: d => d.monthly === wb_ira_monthly ? 1 : 0.5,
      tip: true
    }),
    Plot.dot(
      compound_data.filter(d => d.monthly === wb_ira_monthly && d.year % 5 === 0),
      {x: "year", y: "value", fill: "#f39c12", r: 5, tip: true}
    )
  ]
})
```

### 🎯 Savings Milestones

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
milestone_months = Array.from({length: 61}, (_, i) => i)

milestones_data = milestone_months.map(m => ({
  month: m,
  emergency_1k:  Math.min(wb_savings_amt * 0.35 * m, 1000),
  emergency_3mo: Math.min(wb_savings_amt * 0.35 * m, wb_monthly_net * 3),
  emergency_6mo: Math.min(wb_savings_amt * 0.35 * m, wb_monthly_net * 6)
}))

milestones_tidy = milestones_data.flatMap(d => [
  {month: d.month, value: d.emergency_1k,  series: "$1,000 Starter Fund"},
  {month: d.month, value: d.emergency_3mo, series: "3-Month Emergency Fund ($" + (wb_monthly_net*3).toLocaleString() + ")"},
  {month: d.month, value: d.emergency_6mo, series: "6-Month Emergency Fund ($" + (wb_monthly_net*6).toLocaleString() + ")"}
])

Plot.plot({
  title: "Emergency Fund Build-Up Over Time",
  subtitle: "Saving " + wb_savings_pct + "% of take-home each month toward emergency fund.",
  width: 600,
  height: 300,
  x: {label: "Months →"},
  y: {label: "Emergency Fund Balance ($) →", tickFormat: d => `$${(d/1000).toFixed(0)}k`},
  color: {legend: true},
  marks: [
    Plot.line(milestones_tidy, {
      x: "month", y: "value", stroke: "series",
      strokeWidth: 2, tip: true
    }),
    Plot.ruleY([0])
  ]
})
```

:::

---

## 3. Retirement Accounts Available to Graduate Students

Even contributing $50–$100/month to a Roth IRA in graduate school can grow to $30,000–$50,000 by retirement (assuming 7% average annual return over 35 years).

| Account Type | Who Can Contribute | 2024 Limit | Tax Treatment | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Roth IRA** | Anyone with earned income (W-2 or qualifying fellowship income) under income limits | $7,000/yr ($8,000 if 50+) | Contributions post-tax; growth & withdrawals tax-free | Best option for most grad students (low tax bracket now) |
| **Traditional IRA** | Anyone with earned income | $7,000/yr | Contributions may be deductible; withdrawals taxed | Less advantageous than Roth at low income |
| **403(b)** | University employees (TA/RA) | $23,000/yr (2024) | Pre-tax contributions; taxed on withdrawal | Only if your university offers enrollment |
| **457(b)** | Some university employees | $23,000/yr | Pre-tax; no early withdrawal penalty | Rare but valuable if available |

> **Fellowship students:** IRS guidance on whether fellowship income qualifies as "earned income" for IRA purposes has evolved. As of 2023, qualified stipends can count. Consult [IRS Publication 590-A](https://www.irs.gov/publications/p590a) or a tax professional.

---

## 4. Managing Student Loan Debt

If you carry undergraduate loans into graduate school:

- **Federal loans are typically deferred** during enrollment (in-school deferment), but interest may continue accruing on unsubsidized loans.
- Consider **paying interest only** during graduate school to prevent capitalization.
- Research **income-driven repayment (IDR)** plans: SAVE, PAYE, IBR. These cap payments at a percentage of discretionary income, which can be very low (or $0) on a grad stipend.
- **Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF):** If you plan to work at a nonprofit or government employer after graduation, payments during grad school can count toward the 120 required for PSLF (if you are on an IDR plan and not in deferment). Contact your servicer to verify.

**Tools:**

- [Federal Student Aid Loan Simulator](https://studentaid.gov/loan-simulator/)
- [PSLF Help Tool](https://studentaid.gov/pslf/)

---

## 5. Tax Strategy for Graduate Students

| Strategy | Benefit | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Claim the **Lifetime Learning Credit** | Up to $2,000 credit on tuition & fees | Phases out at higher incomes; cannot be combined with some exclusions |
| Deduct **student loan interest** | Up to $2,500 deduction | Income limits apply |
| Contribute to **Roth IRA** | Tax-free growth | Requires earned income; standard Roth contributions do not require Form 8606 (that form is for nondeductible traditional IRA contributions and Roth conversions) |
| Track **unreimbursed business expenses** (conferences, supplies) | May be deductible as Schedule C expenses if self-employed (fellowship) | Keep receipts |
| File **quarterly estimated taxes** (fellowships) | Avoid underpayment penalty | Use IRS Form 1040-ES |

---

## 6. Building Credit During Graduate School

Good credit saves money on car loans, mortgages, and insurance premiums post-PhD.

- [ ] Open a **no-annual-fee credit card** (e.g., a student card or secured card).
- [ ] Charge a small recurring expense (e.g., streaming subscription) and pay it in full each month.
- [ ] Keep **credit utilization below 30%** of your credit limit.
- [ ] Do **not** close old accounts — length of credit history matters.
- [ ] Check your **free credit reports** annually at [AnnualCreditReport.com](https://www.annualcreditreport.com/).

---

## 7. Additional Resources

- [r/GradSchool (Reddit)](https://www.reddit.com/r/GradSchool/) — community discussions
- [The PhD Student's Guide to Personal Finance (NerdWallet)](https://www.nerdwallet.com/)
- [Bogleheads Wiki: Getting Started](https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started) — low-cost index fund investing
- [Personal Finance for PhDs (blog)](https://pfforphds.com/) — tax and finance advice specifically for graduate students
- [IRS Free File](https://www.irs.gov/filing/free-file-do-your-federal-taxes-for-free) — free federal tax filing

---

## 8. Savings Goal Planner {#sec-savings-goal}

Plan your savings journey toward a major purchase. See how long it will take based on your monthly contribution and savings rate.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sg_goal_type = Inputs.select(["🏠 House Down Payment", "🚗 Car Purchase", "✈️ Other Goal"], {
  label: "Savings Goal",
  value: "🏠 House Down Payment"
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
sg_goal_defaults = {
  "🏠 House Down Payment": {target: 30000, label: "Down payment (10–20% of home price)"},
  "🚗 Car Purchase":       {target: 8000,  label: "Used car cash purchase or down payment"},
  "✈️ Other Goal":         {target: 5000,  label: "Custom savings target"}
}
sg_goal_info = sg_goal_defaults[sg_goal_type]

viewof sg_target = Inputs.select([2000, 3000, 4000, 5000, 6000, 7000, 8000, 10000, 12000, 15000, 18000, 20000, 25000, 30000, 35000, 40000, 50000, 60000, 75000, 100000], {
  label: `Target Amount ($) — ${sg_goal_info.label}`,
  format: d => `$${d.toLocaleString()}`,
  value: sg_goal_info.target
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sg_monthly = Inputs.select([25, 50, 75, 100, 125, 150, 200, 250, 300, 350, 400, 500, 600, 750, 1000], {
  label: "Monthly Savings Contribution ($)",
  format: d => `$${d}/mo`,
  value: 200
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sg_rate = Inputs.select([0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 4.5, 5, 5.5], {
  label: "Annual Interest / HYSA Rate (%)",
  format: d => `${d}%`,
  value: 4.5
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sg_existing = Inputs.select([0, 250, 500, 750, 1000, 1500, 2000, 2500, 3000, 4000, 5000], {
  label: "Existing Savings Toward This Goal ($)",
  format: d => `$${d.toLocaleString()}`,
  value: 0
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Compound savings projection
sg_monthly_rate = sg_rate / 100 / 12

// Future value of existing savings + monthly contributions
sg_months_needed = (() => {
  if (sg_monthly <= 0) return null;
  let balance = sg_existing;
  for (let m = 1; m <= 600; m++) {
    balance = balance * (1 + sg_monthly_rate) + sg_monthly;
    if (balance >= sg_target) return m;
  }
  return null;
})()

sg_projection = Array.from({length: Math.min((sg_months_needed || 240) + 1, 361)}, (_, m) => {
  const growthFactor = Math.pow(1 + sg_monthly_rate, m);
  const principal = sg_existing + sg_monthly * m;
  const balance = sg_existing * growthFactor
    + (sg_monthly_rate > 0 ? sg_monthly * (growthFactor - 1) / sg_monthly_rate : sg_monthly * m);
  const interest = Math.round(Math.max(0, balance - principal));
  return {month: m, balance: Math.round(balance), principal: Math.round(principal), interest};
})

sg_months_text = sg_months_needed
  ? `${sg_months_needed} months (${(sg_months_needed/12).toFixed(1)} years)`
  : "More than 30 years — increase monthly contribution"

sg_final_balance = sg_projection[sg_projection.length - 1]?.balance ?? 0

html`<div style="display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(auto-fit,minmax(130px,1fr));gap:8px;margin:12px 0;">
  <div style="padding:12px 8px;border:2px solid #f39c12;border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.05em;opacity:.6;">Goal</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.3rem;font-weight:700;color:#f39c12;margin-top:3px;">$${sg_target.toLocaleString()}</div>
    <div style="font-size:.72rem;opacity:.65;">${sg_goal_type}</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:12px 8px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.05em;opacity:.6;">Monthly Savings</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.3rem;font-weight:700;margin-top:3px;">$${sg_monthly}/mo</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:12px 8px;border:2px solid ${sg_months_needed ? '#27ae60' : '#e74c3c'};border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.05em;opacity:.6;">Time to Goal</div>
    <div style="font-size:${sg_months_needed ? '1.1' : '.85'}rem;font-weight:700;color:${sg_months_needed ? '#27ae60' : '#e74c3c'};margin-top:3px;">${sg_months_text}</div>
  </div>
  <div style="padding:12px 8px;border:1px solid var(--bs-border-color,#dee2e6);border-radius:8px;text-align:center;">
    <div style="font-size:.7rem;text-transform:uppercase;letter-spacing:.05em;opacity:.6;">Interest Earned</div>
    <div style="font-size:1.3rem;font-weight:700;color:#27ae60;margin-top:3px;">$${(sg_final_balance - (sg_existing + sg_monthly*(sg_projection.length-1))).toLocaleString()}</div>
  </div>
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Savings goal chart
sg_target_line = [{month: 0, balance: sg_target}, {month: sg_projection[sg_projection.length-1].month, balance: sg_target}]

Plot.plot({
  title: `${sg_goal_type} — Savings Projection`,
  subtitle: `$${sg_monthly}/mo at ${sg_rate}% APY, starting with $${sg_existing.toLocaleString()} saved`,
  width: 600,
  height: 300,
  x: {label: "Month →"},
  y: {label: "Balance ($) →", tickFormat: d => `$${(d/1000).toFixed(0)}k`},
  marks: [
    Plot.areaY(sg_projection, {
      x: "month", y: "balance",
      fill: "#27ae60", fillOpacity: 0.12
    }),
    Plot.line(sg_projection, {
      x: "month", y: "balance",
      stroke: "#27ae60", strokeWidth: 2.5, tip: true,
      title: d => `Month ${d.month}: $${d.balance.toLocaleString()}`
    }),
    Plot.line(sg_target_line, {
      x: "month", y: "balance",
      stroke: "#f39c12", strokeWidth: 2, strokeDash: [6, 3]
    }),
    Plot.text([{month: sg_projection[Math.floor(sg_projection.length/2)].month, balance: sg_target}], {
      x: "month", y: "balance", text: d => `🎯 Goal: $${sg_target.toLocaleString()}`,
      dy: -12, fill: "#f39c12", fontWeight: "bold", fontSize: 12
    }),
    sg_months_needed ? Plot.dot(
      sg_projection.filter(d => d.month === sg_months_needed),
      {x: "month", y: "balance", fill: "#27ae60", r: 7, tip: true,
       title: d => `✅ Goal reached at month ${d.month}!\nBalance: $${d.balance.toLocaleString()}`}
    ) : null
  ].filter(Boolean)
})
```

---

*Last reviewed: 2024. Contribution limits and tax rules change annually — verify current figures at [IRS.gov](https://www.irs.gov/).*
